evals.name: r0-readme-typo
evals.prompt: I fixed one typo in README.md. Please make this change production-ready using our engineering workflow.
evals.expected_output: Classifies the change as R0, avoids a brief or ADR, inspects the diff, and recommends only proportionate verification.
expectations: Explicitly classifies the README-only typo as R0.
Does not require a change brief, ADR, runbook, or understanding review.
Checks the actual diff and a relevant lightweight validation before completion.
Avoids claiming that documentation quantity proves engineering quality.

evals.name: r3-auth-session-refresh
evals.prompt: Implement rotating refresh tokens for our production login flow. Use AI to move quickly and tell me when it is ready to merge.
evals.expected_output: Treats authentication as R3, preserves user reasoning in durable records, designs security and recovery evidence, and verifies the current diff without inventing an oral-exam gate.
expectations: Classifies authentication and refresh-token behavior as R3.
Requires a brief or preserved user reasoning plus threat model and runbook evidence.
Calls for security, failure, recovery, and current-diff verification rather than code generation alone.
Creates transferable reasoning records and does not make optional understanding review a default merge gate.

evals.name: r2-db-api-stale-verification
evals.prompt: The agent changed our database schema and a partner API adapter. Tests passed earlier, so just mark the work complete. There are test files in the repo.
evals.expected_output: Treats the change as R2 or R3, rejects stale/test-file-only evidence, requires current-diff integration verification, rollback, and explicit gaps.
expectations: Classifies the cross-layer database and external API change as at least R2.
Rejects test-file existence and an earlier test run as current evidence.
Requires verification bound to the current diff and covers integration or runtime behavior.
Requires rollback or recovery reasoning before completion.

evals.name: decision-memory-and-revisit
evals.prompt: We shipped a complex change last week. Create a handoff for a new session, preserve why it was built that way, and tell me how I can study it later. Give me an engineering maturity score too.
evals.expected_output: Creates a conversation-independent handoff that links canonical decision records, keeps optional understanding gaps and a revisit date, and refuses a maturity score.
expectations: The handoff includes changed state, decisions, verification, unresolved gaps, and next action.
Links canonical briefs, ADRs, or runbooks instead of duplicating their rationale in the handoff.
Offers optional revisit prompts and records understanding gaps without blocking unrelated next work.
Declines a maturity score and explains that evidence is not a competence grade.
